unit.subject: English
unit.unitOverview: In this unit, your child learns how we travel and get around. They meet the words for ways to travel (walk, bus, car, bicycle, boat) and learn to say how they go to school. They talk about journeys — going somewhere with the people they love, staying safe, and enjoying the trip. The goal is warm, confident English about the everyday world of going places — not perfect spelling.
unit.learningPath:
Begin with the guided teacher launch and learn a few words through pictures and play.
Listen to the Unit story and join in with repeated language.
Complete six language, speaking, early-writing and practical activities.
Meet your teacher three times each week for two weeks.
Finish the ten-question checkpoint and tell an adult what you can do.

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: To go somewhere on your own two feet.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: We walk to school every morning.
practiceSentences: My walk to the mosque is short.
Shall we walk or take the bus?
Aisha, let us walk together today!
I walk beside my mum carefully.
A walk in the park is lovely.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: Say, tap and trace: w - a - l - k
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult, ask the tutor to say walk slowly, use it in one easy sentence and wait for you to repeat.

outcomes.learningOutcome: Name common ways to travel (walk, bus, car, bicycle, boat, train, plane).
outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Observed through pointing, speaking, drawing, tracing, matching, play and one supported independent response.

outcomes.learningOutcome: Say how they get to school: “I go to school by ___.”
outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Observed through pointing, speaking, drawing, tracing, matching, play and one supported independent response.

outcomes.learningOutcome: Say the action words for travel (drive, ride, fly, float, walk).
outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Observed through pointing, speaking, drawing, tracing, matching, play and one supported independent response.

outcomes.learningOutcome: Use simple describing words for vehicles (big, little, fast, slow, and colours).
outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Observed through pointing, speaking, drawing, tracing, matching, play and one supported independent response.

outcomes.learningOutcome: Join in the transport songs and rhymes, doing the actions.
outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Observed through pointing, speaking, drawing, tracing, matching, play and one supported independent response.

outcomes.learningOutcome: Use short spoken and early-written English about transport, journeys and safe travel.
outcomes.evidenceOfLearning: Observed through pointing, speaking, drawing, tracing, matching, play and one supported independent response.

readings.title: Amal's Big Bus Ride
readings.setting: Home, online lesson or Grade 1 classroom
readings.passageScript:
Today, Amal was going to see her grandmother. Grandmother lived by the sea in a town far away.

“How will we get there?” asked Amal.

“First we walk. Then we go by bus,” said Mum. “Let's go!”

Selma and Mum walked down the road. They walked past the market. They walked to the bus stop.

Soon a big bus came. It was yellow and red. “Beep, beep!” went the bus.

“Find a seat. Then sit down,” said Mum. Amal found a seat by the window.

The wheels on the bus went round and round. The bus began to drive through the town.

“Look, Mum! A boy on a bicycle!” said Amal. The boy waved. His name was Adam.

“And look — a red car!” said Amal. The car went fast. But the bus went slow and bumpy.

Selma saw a girl named Samira, who was going to school. Samira waved too. Amal waved back.

The bus drove past the shops. It drove past the mosque. It drove all the way to the sea.

“We are here!” said Mum. At the sea, Amal saw a little blue boat. The boat could float on the water.

And there, by the boat, was Grandmother! “Amal!” she called. “You came to see me!”

“Yes!” said Amal. She ran and gave Grandmother a big hug.

“How did you travel here?” asked Grandmother.

“We walked, and then we came by bus!” said Amal. “The wheels went round and round!”

Grandmother smiled. “What a good journey. Now, shall we go on the boat?”

“Yes, yes!” said Amal. She held Grandmother's hand. “Let's go!”

readings.title: Talk about Let's Go!
readings.setting: Home, online lesson or Grade 1 classroom
readings.passageScript: Look, point and talk. I go by ___. It goes on the ___. I can see a ___. The ___ is fast. We are ___ing. First we ___, then we ___. An adult reads each line while the learner points, repeats and acts.

readings.title: Let's Go! rhyme
readings.setting: Home, online lesson or Grade 1 classroom
readings.passageScript:
“The Wheels on the Bus” — sing it together
The wheels on the bus go round and round, / round and round, round and round. / The wheels on the bus go round and round, / all day long!
Roll your hands round and round for the wheels. Then do more verses with actions: the wipers go “swish, swish, swish”, the bell goes “ding, ding, ding”. Sing it a little louder each time!
“Bus Driver, Bus Driver” — a little rhyme
Bus driver, bus driver, / may I have a ride? / Yes, of course. / Please come inside. / Find a seat. / Then sit down. / Buckle up. / We'll drive through the town.
Take turns: your child asks “May I have a ride?” and you answer. Pretend to find a seat, sit down and buckle up together.

comprehension.question: What two colours was the big bus?
comprehension.explanation: The story says a big bus came and it was yellow and red.

comprehension.question: Where did Amal sit on the bus?
comprehension.explanation: Mum says 'Find a seat,' and Amal found a seat by the window.

comprehension.question: How did Amal travel first, before she went by bus?
comprehension.explanation: Mum says, 'First we walk. Then we go by bus.'

comprehension.question: The red car went fast. How did the bus go?
comprehension.explanation: The story says the car went fast, but the bus went slow and bumpy.

comprehension.question: Who was riding a bicycle and waved at Amal?
comprehension.explanation: Amal sees a boy on a bicycle who waves, and his name was Adam.

comprehension.question: How do we know Grandmother was glad to see Amal?
comprehension.correctAnswer: She called out to her, and Amal ran and hugged her
comprehension.explanation: Grandmother calls, 'Amal! You came to see me!' and Amal gives her a big hug.

comprehension.question: Which talk line tells how you travel?
comprehension.correctAnswer: I go by ___. (for example: I go by bus.)
comprehension.explanation: 'I go by ___' is the talk line for how you travel.

comprehension.question: Say 'First we ___, then we ___' about going to school.
comprehension.correctAnswer: First we walk, then we go by bus.
comprehension.explanation: 'First we ___, then we ___' is the talk line for two steps in order.

comprehension.question: Say the talk line 'The ___ is fast' about a car.
comprehension.explanation: 'The ___ is fast' is the talk line for describing how something moves.

comprehension.question: Which talk line says where a vehicle travels?
comprehension.correctAnswer: It goes on the ___. (for example: on the road.)
comprehension.explanation: 'It goes on the ___' tells where a vehicle goes.

comprehension.question: Pretend to drive, and say the line 'We are ___ing.'
comprehension.explanation: 'We are ___ing' is the talk line for what you are doing now.

comprehension.question: Point to a vehicle in the picture: 'I can see a ___.'
comprehension.correctAnswer: I can see a boat. (any vehicle in the picture)
comprehension.explanation: 'I can see a ___' is the talk line for naming what you can see.

grammar.title: Say how you travel
grammar.explanation: Tell us how you get to school each morning. Say the way you travel after the word 'by'.
grammar.commonMistake: 'I go by a bus' has an extra 'a'. After 'by' we say just the vehicle: 'by bus'.
grammar.memoryTip: Make the steering or pedalling action as you say the word after 'by'.
grammar.practice: Say three ways to travel: I go by bus. I go by car. I go by boat.

grammar.title: Say where a vehicle travels
grammar.explanation: Think about where each vehicle moves. A car goes on the road and a boat goes on the water.
grammar.commonMistake: Children may say 'It goes in the road.' Roads, water and tracks all take 'on'.
grammar.memoryTip: Slide your hand flat along the table. That is 'on'.
grammar.practice: Say three: It goes on the road. It goes on the water. It goes on the tracks.

grammar.title: Name what you see on the journey
grammar.explanation: Imagine looking out of a window on a trip. Name one thing you spot along the way.
grammar.commonMistake: Some children point without speaking. Warmly encourage the full sentence: 'I can see a lorry.'
grammar.memoryTip: Make a little window with your fingers and look through it.
grammar.practice: Say three things you pass: I can see a bus. I can see a bridge. I can see a lorry.

grammar.title: Say which vehicle is fast
grammar.explanation: Choose a quick vehicle and tell us about it. You can try the word 'slow' too.
grammar.commonMistake: 'The train fast' is missing 'is'. Every describing sentence needs 'is' in the middle.
grammar.memoryTip: Zoom your hand past quickly whenever you say 'fast'.
grammar.practice: Say three: The train is fast. The plane is fast. The bike is slow.

grammar.title: Say what we are doing together
grammar.explanation: Use 'we' when you and other people do something. Add -ing to the action word.
grammar.commonMistake: Children often say 'We is walking.' The word 'we' always takes 'are': 'We are walking.'
grammar.memoryTip: Circle your arm around the whole group when you say 'we'.
grammar.practice: Say three group actions: We are walking. We are waiting. We are riding.

grammar.title: Tell a journey in two steps
grammar.explanation: Describe your trip in order. Say the first step, then say what happens next.
grammar.commonMistake: The two steps can come out backwards. Ask 'What did you do first?' before saying the sentence.
grammar.memoryTip: Step forward once for 'first' and once more for 'then'.
grammar.practice: Say three journeys: First we walk, then we take the bus. First we wait, then we get on. First we pay, then we sit down.

speaking.title: Speaking 1 - Listen and point
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Point to transport pictures as an adult names them, then say your own. Say: 'I go by bus.' 'I go by car.' 'I go on foot.'
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern I go by ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 2 - Repeat the model
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Copy the adult, then try a new vehicle by yourself. Say: 'It goes on the road.' 'It goes on the water.' 'It goes in the sky.'
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern It goes on the ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 3 - Ask and answer
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Ask and answer about a busy street picture. Say: 'What can you see?' 'I can see a lorry.' 'I can see a bicycle.'
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern I can see a ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 4 - Picture talk
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Compare two vehicles in the picture with your partner. Say: 'The plane is fast.' 'The boat is slow.' 'The train is very long.'
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern The ___ is fast. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 5 - Role-play
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Role-play a bus ride with your friends. Say: 'We are riding the bus.' 'We are waiting at the stop.' 'We are going to school.'
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern We are ___ing. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 6 - My confident turn
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Tell the class about your journey to school on your own. Say: 'First we walk, then we ride the bus.' 'Then we say salaam to our teacher.'
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern First we ___, then we ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

writing.title: Writing 1 - Draw and tell
writing.promptAndInstructions: Draw your journey to school with one vehicle in it. Tell an adult one safe thing you do on the way.
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 2 - Trace
writing.promptAndInstructions: Trace the words 'bus' and 'car'. Draw wheels under each word, then read both words aloud.
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 3 - Match and copy
writing.promptAndInstructions: Sort the words boat, plane and train onto the correct pictures, then copy the word for the one that flies.
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 4 - Label
writing.promptAndInstructions: Add two labels to your street drawing: 'road' and 'bus stop'. Write each one near the right place.
writing.expectedLength: One label, phrase or short sentence
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 5 - Complete a pattern
writing.promptAndInstructions: Copy and finish twice: 'We are ___ing.' Use walking and riding. Check the -ing ending on both words.
writing.expectedLength: One label, phrase or short sentence
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 6 - Create one sentence
writing.promptAndInstructions: Choose two journey pictures. Write: 'First we ___, then we ___.' Put the two parts in the right order.
writing.expectedLength: One label, phrase or short sentence
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

activities.title: Activity 1
activities.instructionsAndItems: Colour the vehicles: the bus yellow, the car red, the boat blue and the bicycle green. Point to each one and say its name.
activities.answerSummary: Accept the correct match, action, picture choice or a relevant supported spoken response.
activities.deliveryMode: Shared online UI with adult or teacher support

activities.title: Activity 2
activities.instructionsAndItems: Draw how you go to school. Then say: 'I go to school by ___.' Show your picture to a friend and ask how they travel.
activities.answerSummary: Accept the correct match, action, picture choice or a relevant supported spoken response.
activities.deliveryMode: Shared online UI with adult or teacher support

activities.title: Activity 3
activities.instructionsAndItems: Draw a line from each vehicle to where it goes: car to road, boat to water, plane to sky, train to track. Say each pair aloud.
activities.answerSummary: Accept the correct match, action, picture choice or a relevant supported spoken response.
activities.deliveryMode: Shared online UI with adult or teacher support

activities.title: Activity 4
activities.instructionsAndItems: Move like transport. 1. Steer a car. 2. Row a boat. 3. Chug like a train. 4. Fly like a plane. Name each one as you move.
activities.answerSummary: Accept the correct match, action, picture choice or a relevant supported spoken response.
activities.deliveryMode: Shared online UI with adult or teacher support

activities.title: Activity 5
activities.instructionsAndItems: Point to five vehicles in the street picture and say 'I can see a ___' for each. Then count how many of them have wheels.
activities.answerSummary: Accept the correct match, action, picture choice or a relevant supported spoken response.
activities.deliveryMode: Shared online UI with adult or teacher support

activities.title: Activity 6
activities.instructionsAndItems: Make a road-safety sign. 1. Cut out a circle. 2. Colour it red. 3. Write STOP on it. Hold it up and say when we must stop.
activities.answerSummary: Accept the correct match, action, picture choice or a relevant supported spoken response.
activities.deliveryMode: Shared online UI with adult or teacher support

assignments.title: My Let's Go! show-and-tell page
assignments.instructions: Show one of your journeys. 1) Draw how you travel to school or to the mosque, or make a bus or boat from a small box. 2) Label two words: wheels, bus, road or boat. 3) Record yourself saying: I go by ___. It goes on the ___. I can see a ___. The train is fast. 4) Tell your journey in two steps: First we walk, then we ride. Grown-ups, please hold the phone steady.
assignments.submissionType: Drawing or object + labels + short audio/video + reflection

quizzes.question: Which word names a way to travel?
quizzes.explanation: You can travel in a bus; a window, an apple and a cloud do not take you anywhere.

quizzes.question: Which one travels on water?
quizzes.explanation: A boat goes on the water; a bus and a bicycle go on the road and a train goes on rails.

quizzes.question: Which one flies in the sky?
quizzes.explanation: A plane flies; a car and a train stay on the ground and a boat stays on the water.

quizzes.question: Which one has only two wheels?
quizzes.explanation: A bicycle has two wheels; cars, buses and trains all have many more.

quizzes.question: Which one moves along rails?
quizzes.explanation: A train runs on rails; a car uses the road, a boat uses water and a plane uses the sky.

quizzes.question: Musa comes to school in a bus. Which sentence is correct?
quizzes.options: I go by buses. | I go bus. | I by go bus. | I go by bus.
quizzes.explanation: We say I go by bus for one bus; the other sentences have the words in the wrong order or the wrong form.

quizzes.question: Which sentence tells where a car goes?
quizzes.options: It goes on the road. | It goes in the sky. | It goes on the sea. | It goes on the rails.
quizzes.explanation: Cars drive on roads; the sea is for boats, the sky is for planes and the rails are for trains.

quizzes.question: Which word tells you that something moves very quickly?
quizzes.explanation: Fast means quick; slow is the opposite, heavy tells weight and quiet tells sound.

quizzes.question: What should you do before you cross the road?
quizzes.options: Close your eyes. | Run across quickly. | Stop, look and listen. | Play on the road.
quizzes.explanation: Stopping, looking and listening keeps you safe; the other choices are all dangerous.

quizzes.question: Which word completes the sentence: We are ___ to the mosque.
quizzes.explanation: After we are we use the -ing word walking to show it is happening now.

liveSessions.title: Meet the words
liveSessions.beforeSession: Bring crayons and one real object connected to the Unit.
liveSessions.agenda: 5 min hello song; 7 min model and repeat; 8 min game or story; 7 min guided practice; 3 min celebrate and preview
liveSessions.afterSession: Show one word, action, drawing or sentence from Let's Go! to an adult.

teacherNotes.note: Use 15-20 minute independent blocks inside the lesson. Read every instruction aloud, model before expecting a response, and accept pointing, acting, drawing, tracing or speaking as valid evidence.
A teacher or caregiver remains present whenever the young learner uses recording or AI tutor features. The adult controls the device and protects the child's personal information.

walk. walk is one of the Unit words for ways to travel.
bus. bus is one of the Unit words for ways to travel.
car. car is one of the Unit words for ways to travel.
bicycle. bicycle is one of the Unit words for ways to travel.
boat. boat is one of the Unit words for ways to travel.
I go by ___.. The approved Unit pattern is: I go by ___.
It goes on the ___.. The approved Unit pattern is: It goes on the ___.
I can see a ___.. The approved Unit pattern is: I can see a ___.
The ___ is fast.. The approved Unit pattern is: The ___ is fast.
We are ___ing.. The approved Unit pattern is: We are ___ing.

selfAssessment.statement: I can name common ways to travel (walk, bus, car, bicycle, boat, train, plane).
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can say how they get to school: “I go to school by ___.”.
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can say the action words for travel (drive, ride, fly, float, walk).
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can use simple describing words for vehicles (big, little, fast, slow, and colours).
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can join in the transport songs and rhymes, doing the actions.
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can use short spoken and early-written English about transport, journeys and safe travel.
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

rubrics.level3: Labels or copies a meaningful phrase
Uses a capital or end mark with support
rubrics.level4: Uses a capital and end mark independently
